title: polylepis incarum
intro: Polylepis incarum, also known as Queñua de los Andes or Q'ente, is a species of flowering plant in the willow family, Salicaceae, native to the Andean highlands of Peru and Bolivia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant compounds. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, fever, and digestive issues, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Traditional herbal preparations of Polylepis incarum include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health concerns.
